audit_items.proof_trace: `data` is declared uninitialized (`char* data;` at L221); `read_file` only writes `*out_data` on success (common.cc:115) and returns `Result::Error` on all failure paths without setting it. When `read_file` fails, the error block at L224-228 calls `delete[] data` on the uninitialized pointer but has no `return`, so execution falls through to the unconditional `delete[] data` at L263, producing a double-delete of an uninitialized pointer (undefined behavior / heap corruption).
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker or user provides a non-existent or unreadable filename; `read_file` returns Error without initializing `data`; `delete[] data` at L227 operates on garbage, then falls through to `delete[] data` at L263 operating on the same garbage value, triggering heap corruption or a crash.
